remaining_gap: helper layer does not resolve unknown-vs-known triggering frame semantics
notes: The frame codec carries the triggering frame type as supplied by the caller; it does not infer an unknown frame type and substitute the zero code on behalf of higher-level endpoint policy.

remaining_gap: raw reason-phrase bytes are preserved without UTF-8 validation
notes: The repository stores CONNECTION_CLOSE reason phrases as byte spans and round-trips them, but it does not currently validate or normalize UTF-8 content.

remaining_gap: packet-number-space and 0-RTT/1-RTT send policy are not modeled here
notes: The helper layer does not yet expose the connection-state and epoch tracking needed to enforce when the application-specific CONNECTION_CLOSE variant may be sent.

remaining_gap: handshake-lifecycle state is not modeled here
notes: The repository does not yet have the endpoint handshake state machine needed to decide when an application may abandon the connection with APPLICATION_ERROR during Initial or Handshake.

notes: Application-protocol error-code usage is already represented by the RESET_STREAM, STOP_SENDING, and application CONNECTION_CLOSE helpers, and is now tagged in-scope.

remaining_gap: duplicate-packet race handling is not modeled by the current packet-processing helpers
notes: The helper layer does not yet implement receiver-side duplicate-discard policy or the timing controls needed to explore this attack surface.

remaining_gap: stateless-reset deployment policy is not modeled as a cluster or topology abstraction
notes: The repository already has stateless-reset helpers, but it does not express the multi-instance deployment policy needed to prove these security requirements end to end.

remaining_gap: future-version downgrade-prevention mechanisms are out of scope for the RFC 9000-specific helper surface
notes: This requirement explicitly speaks about future QUIC versions, so it is not actionable inside the RFC 9000 version-negotiation helpers in this repository.

remaining_gap: connection-scoped authentication and path-state machinery are not present
notes: These requirements depend on a connection-state machine that can compare authenticated packets, track handshake progress, and suppress address changes during the handshake.

remaining_gap: deployment hardening policy is not expressed as code in this repository slice
notes: This is a mitigation recommendation rather than a wire-format helper behavior; it remains out of scope until the repo has deployment-policy surfaces.

remaining_gap: packet-number-skipping telemetry/heuristics are not modeled here
notes: The current codebase does not have a send-path abstraction that could deliberately skip packet numbers as a forgery-detection heuristic.

remaining_gap: request-forgery mitigation policy is deployment-specific and not represented by the helper layer
notes: These are guidance requirements for deployment posture and future extension design; the repository currently has no policy surface to enforce them.
